verificationMode: runtime (adapter-node production build exercised locally via curl)

envForTest.ORIGIN: matches test host so SvelteKit CSRF origin check passes

checks.desc: Unauthenticated GET /api/stats/ gated BEFORE the Django proxy

checks.note: Required a fix: public paths must bypass the pre-existing production tracker redirect, or /login looped to /tracker.

checks.desc: Unauthenticated /_app/ chunk referenced by the login page loads

checks.actual: 404 (no favicon at that path; NOT redirected)

checks.desc: POST /login wrong password (browser form nav: Accept text/html)

checks.expected: 303 Location: /, Set-Cookie sess=<token>.<sig>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax; Max-Age=2592000
checks.actual: 303 Location: /, Set-Cookie sess=...; Max-Age=2592000; Path=/; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax

checks.desc: Request with valid sess cookie passes the auth gate
checks.expected: not 302 to /login; reaches app (tracker redirect for authenticated user is pre-existing behavior)

checks.desc: Cookie with last signature char flipped (timing-safe verify must reject)
GET /logout clears the cookie and redirects
checks.actual: 303 Location: /login, Set-Cookie sess=; Max-Age=0; Path=/; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax

checks.expected: /tracker and / return 200 without any cookie; no SESSION_SECRET warning

outcome: All auth-gate runtime checks passed against the production adapter-node build.
residualNotes: SvelteKit CSRF origin check requires the deployed nginx to pass a correct Host/Origin (standard reverse-proxy setup already documented). Direct-IP POSTs without a matching Origin get 403, which is additional protection, not a regression.
The nginx /api/ block bypasses this gate entirely (see Area D-7 / E-4 doc note). Ops must remove or IP-restrict that block.
